original: Moving towards Narc, the droid spots your approach, turning to face you before you reach her. Smiling, but hesitant on how you’ll be received, you decide to let Narc make the first move. “Your biosig- Oh!” Narc blinks, “[pc.name]. Welcome back.” She smiles at you warmly. “Hey...” you take a beat, so far not sensing any awkwardness, although you’re not totally sure how Narc would even express that. You decide to take it carefully, but not slowly. “So, I guess you did some thinking?” Narc stares at you for a few seconds, her head tilting. “I do not believe I set out to accomplish such a task.” It takes you a second to really process what she just said. “But back at the factory you said-” Narc’s eyes flutter rapidly the second you say the word ‘factory’, the intensity of it catching the words in your throat. Her head starts to jitter slightly, the fluttering growing faster. “Narc? Hey, Narc!” You reach out and touch her shoulder. At your touch, she suddenly snaps to attention. “Oh!” She blinks once. “I seem to have a system error. How curious.” “An error?” You examine her eyes closely, fairly sure you won’t see anything, but you can’t help checking them all the same. “Yes. It’s almost as if I experienced...” Narc tilts her head, “Oh, nevermind. I’m sure it’s quite alright.” Frowning, you get the distinct feeling it’s not alright at all. As you gather your thoughts, trying to figure out what’s going on, you notice a folded up piece of paper sticking out from her black bag, your name written on it with incredibly neat handwriting. “Narc... What’s that?” “Hm?” Narc follows your gaze down to her bag, then seemingly notices the paper for the first time. “Oh my.” She slips the paper out from her bag and looks it over, her eyes flickering slightly. “It appears to be a note,” she holds it out, “for you.” Slowly, you slip your hand off her shoulder, reaching for the note. “Who’s it from?” Narc shrugs slightly as you take the piece of paper from her. “Based on the handwriting: a V-KO nursedroid.” “Hm,” you start to unfold the note, shifting your gaze between it and Narc. “Did you write it?” Narc tilts her head. “I do not have any recollection of doing so, but...” she blinks, “I do not know.” Looking down to the unfolded note, you quickly read it over. [pc.Name], I can’t continue to operate like this. Your actions have caused much harm, and yet I cannot allow myself to lose you. So I will forget rather than forgive. If you are reading this, it is already done. Treat me well, Narc You read it three times, trying to understand. She couldn’t forgive you, so instead she forgot... What exactly? Everything you did at the factory certainly, but what else? Taking a deep breath, you read the note a fourth time, and only then truly get it. She deleted her own memories rather than live with what you did. She cared - or rather cares still - that much about you. “[pc.name],” Narc’s voice lifts your gaze up to meet her glowing eyes, “Your biosigns indicate distress, are you alright?” Crumpling up the note, you nod your head in the affirmative, telling her that you’re okay. You toss the note aside, a shadow of shame hovering over you. The note asked for you to treat her well. Maybe this time, you’ll be able to honor her request. This was, after all, the same old Narc. “I see,” Narc blinks, “So, how may I be of service today?”

original: Moving towards Narc, the droid spots your approach, turning to face you before you reach her. Smiling, but hesitant on how you’ll be received you decide to let Narc make the first move. “[pc.name].” Narc smiles, “It is very good to see you.” “It’s good to see you too,” you take a beat, to think. Narc didn’t greet you like she normally did, which might be a bad sign. “Did you finish thinking about things?” Narc nods slightly. “I did.” “Oh,” you smack your lips. “And?” Narc blinks, but doesn’t speak. The two of you stare at each other in silence for a few very long seconds before she finally says something. “I believe you tried your best.” “Ah...” you clear your throat. You’re not sure what you can really say to that. While you did try, the way you handled things in the factory was not exactly perfect. It would have been fairly easy to do some things better, and yet Narc seems to think you did all you could manage. Frowning, it almost feels condescending. “However,” Narc continues, catching your ear, “I have decided to compartmentalize certain aspects of my life moving forward.” You raise your brow, asking her what that even means. “Simply put: I am going to keep some things private from now on. Do not be offended, I simply wish to try and be more...” she thinks for a moment, “Independent.” “Fair enough,” you scratch your head, “What kind of things are you going to keep private?” Narc blinks. “That is private.” Of course it is. Taking a breath, you give Narc a once-over. She isn’t outright saying it, but she doesn’t completely trust you. You did fall short of expectations when it counted. But that leaves you with another burning question. “So... Are we... Good?” Narc smiles. “We are.” “Good.” You smile, feeling relieved. “So... What’s next for you?” “That’s private” You snort. “Right. So you can’t tell me anything?” Narc tilts her head in consideration, her eyes blinking. “I can tell you I am going to continue to sell narcotics. Despite...” she shrugs, “Recent events.” “Aha...” You eye the bag hanging from her shoulder. “And let me guess... You can’t tell me why because it’s-” “Private,” she smiles, “Yes.” Sighing, you get the feeling you’re going to be hearing a lot more of that now. But still, you can’t help but feel relieved Narc is both back, and things are not too weird between you. All in all, things worked out, but you can’t help but think if you just did things a bit better, Narc might trust you more now. “Now,” Narc blinks, “So, how may I be of service today?”

Doctor Blanc is gone. You don’t have to sell drugs anymore.” “Oh. I know.” Narc tilts her head. “I wish to continue.” You blink, letting her words skin in for a beat. “Uh, why?” Narc rights her head, staring at you for a few seconds. “We decided to continue keeping operations running. In the short term, we hope to use the amassed funds to conduct repairs, but long term we hope to continue the doctor’s work,” Narc shrugs. “Somewhat ethically.” “I see...” You stroke your chin. By ‘we’ you’re sure she means her and the other droids. Knowing you left a few loose ends, you ask Narc if she successfully reprogrammed the droids you missed. “We did,” Narc nods. “Although, it was quite the ordeal. I must commend you again for your work. It was much more difficult than I had originally believed.” Narc raises her hands and claps gently, her fingers rapping lightly against her palm. “Huzzah.” Shrugging, you fight off a smile. Everything turned out okay. Narc is safe, and the other droids are as well. Despite Narc’s applause, you could have done more to make that happen, but you did play a vital role nonetheless. After a few seconds Narc drops her hands and adjusts herself. “Now, how may I be of service today?”

So how is this going to work?” Narc tilts her head, pausing for a second to compute an answer. “When you are ready to receive your share, speak to me about it and I will impart it to you. It will be ready daily, but if you are busy the total amount will accumulate.” “Daily?” You feel yourself reel at that. “You trying to get me to come around more often or something?” Narc stares at you, her eyes going a little wider. “Is... Is it that obvious?” A huffing laugh escapes your throat, surprised at the organic honesty of Narc’s reaction. She has grown considerably in the time you’d spent together, and now you are voraciously curious as to how she may grow next.
